description: Held in the hand, a long pole is 'tolu' when it supports balance, tests the ground, or steadies walking. A ceremonial staff can take the same noun when its pole form matters, but a group's workers or members are named through their roles rather than this English homonym.
articulatory_notes: Touch the tongue tip to the upper teeth for dental 't' and release into stressed mid-back rounded 'o'. Place the tongue tip at the alveolar ridge for lateral 'l', then raise the back of the tongue and keep the lips rounded for 'u'.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: A walking staff supports the body, tests uncertain ground, and can be repaired or replaced from familiar local material.

semantic_domains.activity: a support used while walking or testing the ground
semantic_domains.physical: a long pole held for balance or support
